statement: Oracle harness only. NO interpreter semantics implemented. NO conformance claimed.

spec.role: SEMANTIC ORACLE (authoritative). The official reference interpreter and the official test/core .wast files are the external oracle; we author no expected outputs.

wabt.role: TOOLCHAIN ONLY (format conversion via wast2json). NOT the semantic oracle.

conversion.features_note: Run with WABT's MVP + standardized numeric/local ops, but with the listed post-MVP extensions DISABLED via --disable-* so out-of-scope proposal syntax is rejected at conversion. Two honesty caveats: (1) WABT default features are NOT by themselves an integer guardrail -- SIMD/bulk-memory/reference-types are standardized and ON unless disabled (hence the flags); non-standardized proposals like typed function references are off by default (that is why local_tee.wast fails without --enable-function-references). (2) f32/f64 are MVP-core and cannot be disabled by any flag, so integer-value purity is enforced by MANIFEST CURATION (see targets/excluded), not by conversion flags.

targets_note: Integer-VALUE-clean core files: their value-returning assertions use only i32/i64. (They still contain assert_invalid/assert_malformed validation commands, which the M1 executor will report UNSUPPORTED -- validation is a separate future scope, not a float dependency.)

excluded.name: const.wast
excluded.reason: Contains 300 f32/f64 assert_return commands (float value results) out of 300 assert_returns -- it is a value-type const test, majority float. Outside M0/M1 integer-value scope (NO floating point). Excluded explicitly (not silently skipped); revisit when float value handling enters scope.

excluded.name: local_get.wast
excluded.reason: Contains f32/f64 locals and 5 float assert_return commands (float value results). Outside M0/M1 integer-value scope. Excluded explicitly; revisit with floats.

excluded.name: local_set.wast
excluded.reason: Defines f32/f64 locals in test modules; instantiating/exercising them requires float value representation. Outside M0/M1 integer-value scope. Excluded explicitly; revisit with floats.

excluded.name: local_tee.wast
excluded.reason: At pinned spec commit 82cd4f9, local_tee.wast (lines 615/618) uses typed function-references syntax `(func $f (param (ref null $t)))` / `(ref.null $t)`. It converts only with --enable-function-references, a reference-types proposal outside M0 integer scope (and now also rejected by the --disable-reference-types guardrail). Also carries f32/f64 locals. Excluded EXPLICITLY (not silently skipped) per the no-silent-skipping invariant. Revisit when reference types / floats enter scope.
